channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court Considers Appeal on Matric Result Publication Amid Privacy Concerns
item.description: The South African court is set to rule on the Information Regulator's application for leave to appeal a North Gauteng High Court decision permitting the continued publication of matric results. The ca...

item.title: ACT man arrested in Whitsundays over alleged large-scale drug trafficking
item.description: Jason Adam Reid, a 39-year-old man wanted for alleged large-scale drug trafficking in the ACT, was apprehended by police while hiding in an Airbnb in Hideaway Bay, Queensland. The investigation into R...

item.title: MPs call for quashing of Post Office Capture-era convictions and urgent investigation into justice failures
item.description: The UK government has been urged by the Business and Trade Committee (BTC) to quash convictions obtained using the Post Office's Capture accounting software, a precursor to the flawed Horizon IT syste...

item.title: Durban Labour Court dismisses nurses’ salary upgrade claim against KZN Health Department
item.description: The Durban Labour Court has dismissed an application brought by the Health and Other Service Personnel Trade Union of South Africa (HOSPERSA) on behalf of two KwaZulu-Natal nurses, Priya Naidoo and Si...

item.title: Ottawa Faces Questions Over Non-Disclosure of Iranian Strike on Canadian Military Hub
item.description: Federal officials in Ottawa are facing scrutiny over why the public was not informed about an Iranian strike targeting a military hub in Kuwait that involved Canadian forces. Questions have been raise...

item.title: Government Builds 35 New Neonatal Wards Across All Regions to Reduce Infant Mortality
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania government has started constructing 35 new neonatal wards to boost care for newborn babies in all 26 regions. According to Deputy Health Minister Dr Florence Simizi, 15 of the...

item.title: Winnipeg Selected to Host 2027 Juno Awards Week
item.description: Winnipeg has been confirmed as the host city for the 56th annual Juno Awards, scheduled for April 1-4, 2027. The event will culminate with a gala at Canada Life Centre, broadcast live on CBC. This mar...

item.title: NTSA Announces Speed Cameras Will Be Clearly Visible on Kenyan Roads
item.description: The National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) has announced that all new speed cameras being installed across Kenya's major roads will be clearly marked so that motorists can see them easily. Thi...

item.title: Tinubu Approves More Military Equipment to Strengthen Fight Against Terrorists and Bandits
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don approve fresh military equipment to ginger ongoing operations against terrorists and bandits, especially for areas like Borno wey don see more wahala recently. Defence ...

item.title: Pakistan Conducts Airstrikes on Terrorist Camps in Afghanistan During Operation Ghazab Lil Haq
item.description: In early Friday morning operations, the Pakistani Armed Forces carried out targeted airstrikes on multiple terrorist camps and related support infrastructure in Afghanistan, according to official secu...

item.title: Congress Questions Modi Government's Silence on Ayatollah Khamenei's Death
item.description: On 13th March 2026, Congress leader Jairam Ramesh criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi for remaining silent on the assassination of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Ramesh accused PM Modi of bei...

item.title: Indian stock market slips as West Asia conflict pushes oil prices higher
item.description: The Indian stock market continued to experience a sharp decline on Friday, primarily due to escalating tensions in West Asia and rising oil prices. The benchmark Sensex fell nearly 1% in early trade, ...

item.title: Reflections on the Dunblane School Tragedy 30 Years Later
item.description: On 13 March 1996, Dunblane Primary School in central Scotland witnessed a tragic mass shooting when Thomas Hamilton killed 16 children and their teacher, injuring a further 15 people. The event remain...

item.title: Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission citing role and pay concerns
item.description: Dennis Richardson, former head of ASIO, has stepped down from his role as special adviser to the antisemitism royal commission, citing that he felt 'surplus' to the commission's requirements and that ...

item.title: Bulls and Stormers Brace for Intense Loftus Derby Battle
item.description: Mark Keohane’s column previews the highly anticipated United Rugby Championship derby between the Bulls and the Stormers at Loftus in Pretoria. Originally meant to take place on Boxing Day, the match ...

item.title: Tarriela urges Filipinos to defend West Philippine Sea sovereignty amid disputes
item.description: Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela, the spokesperson of the Philippine Coast Guard for the West Philippine Sea, reminded Filipinos to stand united in defending the country’s maritime rights and sovereignty. In...

item.title: India co-sponsors UN Security Council resolution condemning Iran's attacks on GCC nations
item.description: New Delhi: India has co-sponsored a United Nations Security Council (UNSC) resolution that condemns Iran for carrying out attacks against Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries and Jordan. The resol...

item.title: Parliament Urges Hospitals to Strengthen Maternal and Child Health Services
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Health and AIDS has directed all hospitals across Tanzania to strictly follow Parliament's guidelines on improving mother and child health progr...

item.title: Independent Commission for Infrastructure to conclude operations on March 31 after six months
item.description: After six months of operation, the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI), established to investigate a decade of multi-billion-peso corruption in public infrastructure projects, announced th...

item.title: Long Island Teen Found Dead in Brooklyn After Missing Since January
item.description: Thomas Medlin, a 15-year-old from St. James, Long Island, was found dead in Brooklyn waters on March 7, more than two months after he went missing. Medlin disappeared on January 9 after leaving the St...
